title: Adding 24 hours is not the same as adding one calendar day across a DST transition
rule: A fixed duration (e.g. +86,400,000 ms) advances the absolute instant, so on a spring-forward day the wall-clock time shifts by an hour (noon becomes 1pm); a calendar operation (setDate(+1), dateutil relativedelta, Period.plusDays) keeps the same wall-clock time but the elapsed duration is 23 or 25 hours. Pick deliberately: use duration math for 'how much time elapsed' and calendar math for 'same time tomorrow'. Mixing them produces off-by-one-hour bugs only on DST-transition days, so they survive most tests.
triggers: time is off by one hour the day after DST
adding 24 hours gives wrong clock time
setDate plus one does not match plus 86400000
scheduled job fires an hour early after clocks change
spring forward fall back off by one hour
